message.content:
Fix BLOCKER/MAJOR issues in /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora_java/workspace/java-lld-movieticket-001_lld/.

Issues:
1. [MAJOR] BookingService.holdSeats (src/main/java/com/interview/lld/moviebooking/service/BookingService.java:77-114) + ShowSeat.hold:56: Stranded-seat LEAK on duplicate seat labels — the exact failure the two-phase commit claims to prevent. A request like holdSeats("show-1", ["A1","A1"]) passes phase 1 (isAvailable() returns true for A1 twice), then phase 2 calls seat.hold() on A1 (flips it to HELD), then calls hold() on A1 AGAIN, which throws InvalidBookingException("Cannot hold seat A1 in status HELD") from inside ShowSeat. holdSeats only anticipates SeatsUnavailableException, so this throw propagates uncaught: A1 is left HELD, NO hold is recorded in the registry. Verified empirically: after the throw, HELD=1 but liveHoldCount=0, and advancing the clock 30 min + sweepExpiredHolds() releases 0 — the seat is PERMANENTLY stranded (no hold id exists to cancel/confirm/sweep). This directly falsifies the writeup's headline claim (§5.5 D: "every seat passed, so this cannot fail midway" / "no stranded seats"). Happens identically under the per-showtime lock in ThreadSafeBookingService.
   Fix: Deduplicate/validate the seat-label set before phase 2 (e.g. reject duplicates in validateSeatRequest with InvalidBookingException, or build resolveSeats over a LinkedHashSet). Alternatively wrap phase 2 so any hold() failure rolls back already-held seats in this request.

Fix CODE (not test unless test itself is weak). Keep Java 17+ idioms, one base exception, no ... bodies. After fix, run mvn test, confirm green. Update §7 in _response.md if output changed. Return what changed + final test status.

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.

TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

content.text: Let me look at the SeatHold model and the test file to understand idioms and existing test coverage.

Now I understand the codebase fully. The cleanest fix is to reject duplicate seat labels in `validateSeatRequest` with `InvalidBookingException`. This stays consistent with the design philosophy: caller mistakes (empty list, unknown labels) are rejected with a clear error before any mutation. A duplicate seat label in a single request is a caller mistake too.

Let me apply the fix to `validateSeatRequest`.

905	    return showtime;
906	}

908	private String requireShowtimeIdForHold(String holdId) {
909	    SeatHold hold = holds.get(holdId);
910	    if (hold == null) throw new InvalidBookingException("Unknown or already-used hold: " + holdId);
911	    return hold.showtimeId();
912	}
913	
914	private void validateSeatRequest(Showtime showtime, List<String> seatLabels) {
915	    if (seatLabels == null || seatLabels.isEmpty())
916	        throw new InvalidBookingException("Must request at least one seat");
917	    for (String label : seatLabels)
918	        if (showtime.getSeat(label) == null)
919	            throw new InvalidBookingException("Unknown seat " + label
920	                + " for showtime " + showtime.getShowtimeId());
921	}
922	
923	// release every seat a hold covers — used by cancel, the expiry guard, and the sweeper
924	private void releaseHold(SeatHold hold) {
925	    Showtime showtime = showtimes.get(hold.showtimeId());
926	    if (showtime == null) return;
927	    for (String label : hold.seatLabels()) {
928	        ShowSeat seat = showtime.getSeat(label);
929	        if (seat != null) seat.release();
930	    }
931	}
932	```
933	
934	`releaseHold` is the canonical "put seats back" path — cancel, the confirm expiry guard, and the sweeper all funnel through it, so the rule lives in exactly one place.
935	
936	### Verification (full-lifecycle dry runs)
937	
938	**Scenario 1 — Happy path** (clock 18:00; seats A1..A5 all AVAILABLE)
939	
940	| Step | Call | After |
941	|---|---|---|
942	| 1 | `holdSeats("show-1", [A1,A2])` | A1,A2: HELD; `holds={hold-1}`; expiry 18:10 |
943	| 2 | `confirmBooking("hold-1")` | A1,A2: BOOKED; `holds={}`; Ticket tkt-1 returned |
944	
945	Both registries consistent; two seats booked. ✓
946	
947	**Scenario 2 — Expired pickup**
948	
949	| Step | Call | After |
950	|---|---|---|
951	| 1 | `holdSeats("show-1", [A3,A4])` | A3,A4: HELD; expiry 18:10 |
952	| 2 | clock.advance(11 min) → now 18:11 | (no state change) |
953	| 3 | `sweepExpiredHolds()` | A3,A4: AVAILABLE; `holds={}`; returns 1 |
954	| 4 | `confirmBooking("hold-...")` | throws `InvalidBookingException` (hold gone) ✓ |
955	
956	**Scenario 3 — Race (plain English):** two threads call `holdSeats("show-1", [A2,A3])` and `holdSeats("show-1", [A3,A4])` at the same instant. WITHOUT a lock both pass phase-1 on A3 and both hold it — A3 is double-booked. WITH the per-showtime lock, one thread holds the lock through its whole check-and-hold; the second waits, then its phase-1 sees A3 already HELD and it throws `SeatsUnavailableException(["A3"])`. Exactly one wins.
957	
958	### Complexity Summary
959	
960	| Operation | Time | Space | Key reason |
961	|---|---|---|---|
962	| `holdSeats` | O(k) | O(k) | k = seats requested; two passes over a tiny list |
963	| `confirmBooking` | O(k) | O(1) | O(1) hold lookup + confirm each of k seats |
964	| `cancelHold` | O(k) | O(1) | O(1) lookup + release k seats |
965	| `sweepExpiredHolds` | O(h·k) | O(h) | h = live holds; snapshot then release each expired |
966	| `countByStatus` | O(s) | O(1) | s = seats in the showtime |
967	
968	---
969	
970	## 7. Putting It Together (verified run + JUnit suite)
971	
972	### 7.1 `Main.java` — narrated demo (ACTUAL output)
973	
974	Command:
975	```
976	mvn -q compile exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="com.interview.lld.moviebooking.Main"
977	```
978	
979	Output:
980	```
981	Case 1 atomic multi-seat booking -> OK (ticket tkt-1)
982	Case 2 partial conflict rejected all-or-nothing -> OK
983	Case 3 unpaid hold expired after 10 min -> OK (released 1)
984	Case 4 invalid inputs rejected -> OK
985	Case 5 stampede: 40 threads, exactly 1 won -> OK
986	ALL CASES PASSED
987	```
988	
989	The five cases cover: atomic multi-seat booking, all-or-nothing rejection on a partial conflict, 10-minute hold expiry via the sweeper + a rejected late confirm, boundary input validation, and a 40-thread stampede where exactly one thread wins the contested seats.
990	
991	### 7.2 JUnit 5 suite (ACTUAL output)
992	
993	Command:
994	```
995	mvn test
996	```
997	
998	Output:
999	```
1000	[INFO] Tests run: 13, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0
1001	[INFO] BUILD SUCCESS
1002	```
1003	
1004	The 13 tests cover every public method (happy paths), every typed exception (`UnknownShowtimeException`, `InvalidBookingException`, `SeatsUnavailableException`), the all-or-nothing back-out, expiry via sweep and via the confirm-time guard, idempotent cancel, and TWO concurrency tests.
1005	
1006	**The strict concurrency test** (`concurrentOverlappingHoldsNeverDoubleBook`): 50 threads all request the SAME overlapping pair {S1, S2} behind a `CyclicBarrier` so they hit the critical section simultaneously. Assertions:
1007	- `wins == 1` — exactly one hold succeeded
1008	- `winningHoldIds.size() == 1` — the winner has a unique hold id
1009	- `conflicts == 49` — every loser got the TYPED `SeatsUnavailableException` (no surprise exceptions)
1010	- post-state: exactly 2 seats HELD, 2 AVAILABLE, 1 live hold
1011	
1012	A second test (`concurrentDisjointHoldsAllSucceed`) proves 4 threads requesting disjoint seats ALL succeed in parallel — confirming the per-showtime lock doesn't needlessly serialize independent bookings within a showtime.
1013	
1014	---

844	    }

848	**5. Reasoning:** this is the two-phase commit at the center of the design. Phase 1 walks every requested seat and *collects* conflicts rather than throwing on the first one — so the error message can name every blocked seat. Phase 2 only runs if phase 1 found zero conflicts, which means no `seat.hold()` can ever fail partway and strand a seat. Notice we do NOT validate seat existence inside the loop — that's `validateSeatRequest`'s job, run before the lock so the critical section stays tiny. The `gateLock`/`gateUnlock` pair brackets exactly the check-and-hold; everything before it reads immutable data and is safe outside the lock.
849	
850	> 🎙️ Say: "Check all, then hold all. If any seat is taken, I throw before touching anything — all-or-nothing."
851	> ⚠️ "Two users want overlapping seats at the same instant?" → "In §9 the whole check-and-hold runs under a per-showtime lock, so only one gets through; the other sees the seat held and gets a typed error."
852	
853	#### Method — `confirmBooking` (redemption)

input.old_string: **5. Reasoning:** this is the two-phase commit at the center of the design. Phase 1 walks every requested seat and *collects* conflicts rather than throwing on the first one — so the error message can name every blocked seat. Phase 2 only runs if phase 1 found zero conflicts, which means no `seat.hold()` can ever fail partway and strand a seat. Notice we do NOT validate seat existence inside the loop — that's `validateSeatRequest`'s job, run before the lock so the critical section stays tiny. The `gateLock`/`gateUnlock` pair brackets exactly the check-and-hold; everything before it reads immutable data and is safe outside the lock.
input.new_string: **5. Reasoning:** this is the two-phase commit at the center of the design. Phase 1 walks every requested seat and *collects* conflicts rather than throwing on the first one — so the error message can name every blocked seat. Phase 2 only runs if phase 1 found zero conflicts, which means no `seat.hold()` can ever fail partway and strand a seat — **provided every target seat is distinct**. That distinctness is exactly what `validateSeatRequest` guarantees: it rejects duplicate labels (a request like `[A1,A1]` would otherwise pass phase-1 twice while A1 is free, then have phase-2 hold A1 once and re-hold it — the second `hold()` throws and strands the seat with no hold recorded). Notice we do NOT validate seat existence or uniqueness inside the phase loop — that's `validateSeatRequest`'s job, run before the lock so the critical section stays tiny. The `gateLock`/`gateUnlock` pair brackets exactly the check-and-hold; everything before it reads immutable data and is safe outside the lock.

input.summary:
Fixed the [MAJOR] stranded-seat leak on duplicate seat labels in java-lld-movieticket-001_lld by rejecting duplicates before the two-phase commit ever mutates state.

WHAT CHANGED (code):
- /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora_java/workspace/java-lld-movieticket-001_lld/src/main/java/com/interview/lld/moviebooking/service/BookingService.java
  - validateSeatRequest now tracks seen labels in a HashSet<String> and throws InvalidBookingException("Duplicate seat <label> in request") when a label repeats. Because validateSeatRequest runs BEFORE gateLock and before any seat is touched, the request backs out cleanly with no mutation — so a request like holdSeats("show-1", ["A1","A1"]) can never reach phase 2 and re-call seat.hold() on an already-HELD seat. This eliminates the uncaught InvalidBookingException-from-ShowSeat path that left A1 HELD with no hold recorded (HELD=1, liveHoldCount=0, unsweepable). Added imports java.util.HashSet and java.util.Set. Used HashSet (not LinkedHashSet) since order is irrelevant for a membership check. Fix is inherited unchanged by ThreadSafeBookingService (it only overrides gateLock/gateUnlock), so the per-showtime-lock path is fixed identically. Kept one base exception (InvalidBookingException extends BookingException), Java 17 idioms, no stub bodies.

WHAT CHANGED (test — strengthened, not weakened):
- /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora_java/workspace/java-lld-movieticket-001_lld/src/test/java/com/interview/lld/moviebooking/BookingServiceTest.java
  - Added regression test duplicateSeatLabelRejectedAndTouchesNothing: asserts the duplicate request throws InvalidBookingException, then asserts AVAILABLE=5, HELD=0, liveHoldCount=0 (no stranded seat), then proves A1 can still be held normally and that a real cancelable hold id exists afterward (the bug had none).

WHAT CHANGED (writeup §7 / §5):
- /Users/gbang/Downloads/algora_java/workspace/java-lld-movieticket-001_lld/_response.md
  - §7.2 updated test count 13 -> 14 in both the actual-output block and the prose, and noted the new duplicate-seat-label coverage.
  - §5 holdSeats reasoning corrected: the "no seat.hold() can fail midway / no stranded seats" claim now explicitly states it holds *provided every target seat is distinct*, and credits validateSeatRequest's duplicate rejection as the precondition that makes it true.
  - validateSeatRequest code snippet in the writeup updated to show the dedup logic with an explanatory comment.

TEST STATUS: mvn test -> Tests run: 14, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0, BUILD SUCCESS (up from 13 before; the new test fails against the old code and passes with the fix).

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
Fixed the MAJOR stranded-seat leak. The root cause was that `holdSeats` validated existence and emptiness but not uniqueness, so a duplicate label like `["A1","A1"]` passed phase 1 twice (A1 still free both checks), then phase 2 held A1 and re-held it — the second `ShowSeat.hold()` threw an uncaught `InvalidBookingException`, leaving A1 permanently HELD with no hold recorded.

The fix rejects duplicates in `validateSeatRequest` (which runs before the lock and before any mutation), so the offending request can never reach phase 2. All 14 tests pass (added one regression test; was 13).
